title: Segmentation fault in hierarchy pass
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Running the command
```
yosys -p 'read_verilog top.v' -p 'hierarchy -top top -libdir .'
```

with the following files:

`top.v`
```
module top();
wire array[0:0];
wire out;
sub #(.d(1)) inst(
    .in(array[0]),
    .out(out)
);
endmodule
```

`sub.v`:
```
module sub #(parameter d=1) (input in, output out);
assign out = in;
endmodule
```

(The example is minimized, we couldn't simplify anything more without making the segfault disappear.)

## Expected behavior

Not segfaulting ;)

## Actual behavior

```shell
$  yosys ~/tools/yosys/yosys -p 'read_verilog top.v' -p 'hierarchy -top top -libdir .'

 /----------------------------------------------------------------------------\
 |                                                                            |
 |  yosys -- Yosys Open SYnthesis Suite                                       |
 |                                                                            |
 |  Copyright (C) 2012 - 2020  Claire Xenia Wolf <claire@yosyshq.com>         |
 |                                                                            |
 |  Permission to use, copy, modify, and/or distribute this software for any  |
 |  purpose with or without fee is hereby granted, provided that the above    |
 |  copyright notice and this permission notice appear in all copies.         |
 |                                                                            |
 |  THE SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED "AS IS" AND THE AUTHOR DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES  |
 |  WITH REGARD TO THIS SOFTWARE INCLUDING ALL IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF          |
 |  MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE AUTHOR BE LIABLE FOR   |
 |  ANY SPECIAL, DIRECT, INDIRECT, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES OR ANY DAMAGES    |
 |  WHATSOEVER RESULTING FROM LOSS OF USE, DATA OR PROFITS, WHETHER IN AN     |
 |  ACTION OF CONTRACT, NEGLIGENCE OR OTHER TORTIOUS ACTION, ARISING OUT OF   |
 |  OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE USE OR PERFORMANCE OF THIS SOFTWARE.            |
 |                                                                            |
 \----------------------------------------------------------------------------/

 Yosys 0.20+54 (git sha1 060cbd3e9, gcc 9.4.0-1ubuntu1~20.04.1 -fPIC -Os)


-- Running command `read_verilog top.v' --

1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: top.v
Parsing Verilog input from `top.v' to AST representation.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\top'.
Successfully finished Verilog frontend.

-- Running command `hierarchy -top top -libdir .' --

2. Executing HIERARCHY pass (managing design hierarchy).

2.1. Analyzing design hierarchy..
Top module:  \top

2.2. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: ./sub.v
Parsing Verilog input from `./sub.v' to AST representation.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\sub'.
Successfully finished Verilog frontend.
Reprocessing module top because instantiated module sub has become available.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\top'.

2.3. Analyzing design hierarchy..
Top module:  \top
Used module:     \sub
Parameter \d = 1

2.4. Executing AST frontend in derive mode using pre-parsed AST for module `\sub'.
Parameter \d = 1
Generating RTLIL representation for module `$paramod\sub\d=s32'00000000000000000000000000000001'.
Reprocessing module top because instantiated module $paramod\sub\d=s32'00000000000000000000000000000001 has become available.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\top'.

2.5. Analyzing design hierarchy..
Top module:  (null)
Used module:     $paramod\sub\d=s32'00000000000000000000000000000001
[1]    2463504 segmentation fault (core dumped)  ~/tools/yosys/yosys -p 'read_verilog top.v' -p 'hierarchy -top top -libdir .'
```
